seoTitle: Deua vs Devils Hole — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Deua vs Devils Hole: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Deua and Devils Hole.
heading: Deua vs Devils Hole — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Deua, New South Wales and Devils Hole, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devils Hole has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Deua (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Deua or Devils Hole safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Deua has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Devils Hole scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Deua scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Devils Hole scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Deua or Devils Hole?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devils Hole has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
